title: fieldClim: Workflow-Schritte für Energiebilanz und Wärmeflussmethoden
text: Interpretation. Diese Grafik trennt bewusst die energiegebundenen beziehungsweise residualen Pfade von Monin-Obukhov/Profile. Bei Bulk-Residual, PT und Bowen bedeutet Nähe zur Nulllinie: Die Summe aus L und V passt zur verwendeten verfügbaren Energie. Bei PT ist das konstruktiv, bei Bulk-Residual für gültige Zeitschritte ebenfalls, weil V als Rest berechnet wird. Bowen kann ebenfalls exakt schließen, solange der Nenner nicht geguardet oder nicht endlich ist. Diese Nulllinie beweist aber nicht, dass jede einzelne Partitionierung physikalisch gut ist. Sie sagt nur: Die Energiebuchhaltung ist konsistent. Ob die Aufteilung L und V plausibel ist, muss zusätzlich über Gradienten, Extremwerte und Warnungen gelesen werden.
code: cols_closure <- c(   "#666666",   "#CC79A7",   "#009E73" )  closure_values <- c(   caldern$diff_bulk_pkg,   caldern$diff_pt,   caldern$diff_bowen )  ylim_closure <- range(closure_values, na.rm = TRUE)  op <- par(mar = c(6, 4, 3, 1))  plot(   caldern$datetime,   caldern$diff_bulk_pkg,   type = "l",   col = cols_closure[1],   lwd = 2,   ylim = ylim_closure,   xlab = "Zeit",   ylab = "(L + V) - (Q* - B) [W/m²]",   main = "Energetische Konsistenz: bilanzgebundene Pfade" )  lines(caldern$datetime, caldern$diff_pt, col = cols_closure[2], lwd = 2) lines(caldern$datetime, caldern$diff_bowen, col = cols_closure[3], lwd = 2) abline(h = 0, lty = 2, col = "grey40")  par(xpd = NA) legend(   "bottom",   inset = c(0, -0.35),   horiz = TRUE,   bty = "n",   legend = c(     "Bulk Paket ri_guard",     "Priestley-Taylor",     "Bowen"   ),   col = cols_closure,   lty = 1,   lwd = 2 ) par(op)

title: fieldClim: Workflow-Schritte für Energiebilanz und Wärmeflussmethoden
text: Interpretation. Diese Grafik muss anders gelesen werden als die vorige. Monin-Obukhov/Profile berechnet \(L\) und \(V\) aus den gemessenen Unterschieden zwischen zwei Höhen: Temperatur, Feuchte, Wind und Messhöhe bestimmen das Ergebnis. Die Werte werden nicht nachträglich angepasst, dass ihre Summe genau der verfügbaren Energie \(Q^* - B\) entspricht. Die Paketfunktionen fangen inzwischen problematische Sonderfälle ab: Nullgradienten führen für den jeweiligen Fluss zu 0, ungültige Höhen oder Windwerte sowie nicht auswertbare Profilzustände führen zu NA mit Warnung. Große, aber rechnerisch gültige Werte bleiben jedoch sichtbar. Deshalb zeigt die Grafik nicht einfach einen Fehler, sondern prüft, ob die profilbasierten Werte energetisch zur verfügbaren Energie passen. Wenn die Abweichung groß ist, sollte der entsprechende Zeitschritt nicht als belastbarer Wärmefluss interpretiert werden. Wahrscheinlicher ist dann, dass kleine Temperatur- oder Feuchtegradienten, schwache Windunterschiede oder Stabilitätsannahmen den Profilansatz stark beeinflussen. Die Randmarker sind hier wichtig. Sie bedeuten: Einige Werte liegen außerhalb der robust dargestellten Achse. Die Grafik zeigt also nicht „kleine Abweichungen“, sondern eine lesbare Mitte der Verteilung plus Hinweise auf Ausreißer. Das lässt sich übersetzen: Der Profilpfad sagt nicht „viel Energie wurde bilanziert“, sondern „reagiert eine profil- und stabilitätsabhängige Methode auf diese Messprofile“. Wenn diese Reaktion stark von Q_star - B abweicht, ist das eine Diagnose über Gradienten, Windschere und Stabilitätsannahmen.
code: mo_diff <- caldern$diff_monin mo_finite <- is.finite(mo_diff) mo_ylim <- quantile(mo_diff[mo_finite], probs = c(0.05, 0.95), na.rm = TRUE)  mo_lower <- mo_finite & mo_diff < mo_ylim[1] mo_upper <- mo_finite & mo_diff > mo_ylim[2] mo_inside <- mo_finite & !mo_lower & !mo_upper  op <- par(mar = c(6, 4, 3, 1))  plot(   caldern$datetime[mo_inside],   mo_diff[mo_inside],   type = "l",   col = "#D55E00",   lwd = 2,   ylim = mo_ylim,   xlab = "Zeit",   ylab = "(L_MO + V_MO) - (Q* - B) [W/m²]",   main = "Monin-Obukhov/Profile: Plausibilitätsprüfung gegen Q* - B" )  abline(h = 0, lty = 2, col = "grey40") points(caldern$datetime[mo_lower], rep(mo_ylim[1], sum(mo_lower)),        pch = 25, col = "#D55E00", bg = "#D55E00") points(caldern$datetime[mo_upper], rep(mo_ylim[2], sum(mo_upper)),        pch = 24, col = "#D55E00", bg = "#D55E00")  mtext(   paste0(     "Randmarker: ",     sum(mo_lower), " unten, ", sum(mo_upper), " oben"   ),   side = 3,   line = 0.2,   cex = 0.8,   col = "grey30" ) par(op)

title: fieldClim: Workflow-Schritte für Energiebilanz und Wärmeflussmethoden
text: Die Methoden reagieren auf denselben Stationsdatensatz unterschiedlich, weil sie nicht dieselbe Frage beantworten. Für diesen Caldern-Tag ergibt sich daraus eine klare Lesart: Priestley-Taylor ist der stabile Einstiegspfad. Er beantwortet: Wie lässt sich die verfügbare Energie ohne empfindliche Profilquotienten L und V aufteilen? Bulk-Residual mit ri_guard beantwortet: Welche Zeitschritte erlauben eine einfache wind- und temperaturgradientenbasierte Schätzung von L, und wo sagt die Stabilitätsdiagnose, dass der neutrale Bulk-Ansatz nicht belastbar ist? Bowen beantwortet: Wie würde ein Temperatur-/Feuchtegradient die verfügbare Energie partitionieren? Die Methode kann energetisch schließen und trotzdem einzelne extreme Anteile erzeugen. Penman beantwortet nur die Frage nach V. Es ist kein vollständiges L/V-Paar und darf deshalb nicht wie PT, Bulk oder Bowen bilanziell verglichen werden. Monin-Obukhov/Profile beantwortet eine Profilfrage: ergeben Temperatur-, Feuchte- und Windunterschiede zwischen zwei Höhen, wenn sie als turbulenter Austausch interpretiert werden? Große Ausschläge, NA-Werte oder Abweichungen von \(Q^* - B\) sind deshalb keine bloßen Störungen. Sie zeigen, dass Gradienten, Windschere oder Stabilitätsannahmen für diese Zeitschritte kritisch sind. Die wichtigste praktische Konsequenz lautet: Der Methodenvergleich ist keine Suche nach der einen „besten“ Kurve. Er zeigt, welche Annahmen bei denselben Stationsdaten stabil sind und welche Annahmen zu Diagnosefällen führen. Für eine robuste Auswertung sollte PT als Baseline gelesen werden, Bulk-Residual mit Guard als transparenter residualer Vergleich, Bowen als gradientenempfindliche Partitionierung, Penman als separater V-Vergleich und Monin-Obukhov/Profile als Diagnosepfad.

text: Latent heat flux W m-2.

title: Latent Heat using Bowen Method — latent_bowen
text: latent heat flux (\(Q_e\)) using Bowen method calculated : $$Q_e = \frac{R_n - G}{1 + B}$$ : \(R_n\) net radiation, \(G\) soil heat flux, \(B\) Bowen ratio. implemented Bowen ratio (\(B\)) calculated potential-temperature gradient absolute-humidity gradient: $$B = \gamma_{code} \cdot \frac{\Delta \theta / \Delta z}{\Delta AH / \Delta z}$$ : \(\gamma_{code} = 0.00066 \cdot (1 + 0.000946 \cdot t_1)\) empirical implementation coefficient; exact source-form equivalence remains source-open, \(\theta\) potential temperature, \(AH\) absolute humidity. inputs t1 t2 converted potential temperature temperature gradient formed. inputs hum1 hum2 relative humidity values converted internally absolute humidity humidity gradient formed. \(1 + B\) close zero, latent heat flux can become unrealistically high. cap parameter numerical safeguard replaces near-zero denominators +/- cap. Exact closure sensible_bowen() guaranteed finite uncapped denominators; capped cases guarded diagnostic outputs may close rad_bal - soil_flux exactly. Non-finite Bowen ratios denominators return NA affected elements warning.

title: Latent heat flux as residual of the surface energy balance — latent_bulk_residual
text: package energy-balance convention : $$ R_n = G + H + LE $$ storage omitted. \(R_n\) net radiation, \(G\) soil heat flux, \(H\) sensible heat flux, \(LE\) latent heat flux. implemented residual : $$ LE_{res} = R_n - G - H $$ \(LE_{res}\) latent heat flux W m-2, \(R_n\) net radiation W m-2, \(G\) soil heat flux W m-2, \(H\) sensible heat flux W m-2. sign convention : \(R_n > 0\): radiative energy input surface. \(G > 0\): heat flux soil. \(H > 0\): sensible heat flux away surface. \(LE > 0\): latent heat flux away surface. Bulk-Residual workflow, sensible_bulk() first estimates \(H_{bulk}\). latent heat flux calculated : $$ LE_{res} = R_n - G - H_{bulk} $$ Therefore Bulk-Residual workflow closes available energy construction: $$ H_{bulk} + LE_{res} = R_n - G $$ closure algebraic. prove \(H_{bulk}\) physically perfect sensible-heat estimate. error \(R_n\), \(G\), \(H_{bulk}\) inherited residual latent heat flux. Large absolute residuals warned using warn_threshold, capped.

text: Latent heat flux W m-2.

title: Latent Heat using Monin-Obukhov length — latent_monin
text: latent heat flux (\(Q_e\)) using Monin-Obukhov method calculated : $$Q_e = -\rho \cdot L_v \cdot \frac{k \cdot u_*}{\phi_q} \cdot \frac{\Delta q}{\Delta z}$$ : \(\rho\) air density, \(L_v\) latent heat vaporization, \(k\) von Karman constant, \(u_*\) friction velocity, \(\phi_q\) stability correction function humidity, \(\Delta q\) moisture gradient, \(\Delta z\) height difference measurements. stability correction function humidity (\(\phi_q\)) calculated using gradient Richardson number (\(Ri_g\)) stability parameter (\(s_1\)). stability parameter (\(s_1\)) ratio upper measurement height Monin-Obukhov length. Monin-Obukhov length close zero, ratio can become excessively large, leading unrealistic values. address , stability parameter (\(s_1\)) can capped maximum absolute value. Invalid heights, invalid wind speeds, invalid numerical profile states guarded elementwise return NA warning. Zero moisture gradient returns zero latent heat flux. default cap set NULL. $$\phi_q = \begin{cases} 0.95 \cdot (1 - 11.6 \cdot s_1)^{-0.5}, & \text{} Ri_g \leq 0 \\ 0.95 + 7.8 \cdot s_1, & \text{} Ri_g > 0 \end{cases}$$

text: Latent heat flux W m-2.

title: Latent Heat Penman-Monteith-Type Method — latent_penman
text: latent heat flux (\(Q_e\)) using simplified Penman-Monteith-type method calculated : $$Q_e = \frac{\Delta (R_n - G) + \gamma \frac{c_p \rho}{r_a} (e_s - e_a)}{\Delta + \gamma (1 + \frac{r_s}{r_a})}$$ : \(\Delta\) slope saturation vapor pressure curve, \(\gamma\) psychrometric constant, \(R_n\) net radiation, \(G\) soil heat flux, \(c_p\) specific heat air, \(\rho\) air density, \(r_a\) aerodynamic resistance, \(r_s\) surface resistance, \(e_s\) saturation vapor pressure, \(e_a\) actual vapor pressure. pres_sat_vapor_p() pres_vapor_p() return pressure hPa. latent_penman() converts \(e_s\) \(e_a\) internally kPa computing aerodynamic vapour-pressure-deficit term. \(\Delta\) \(\gamma\) handled kPa scale. aerodynamic resistance (\(r_a\)) calculated based wind speed, observation height, surface roughness. surface resistance (\(r_s\)) selected based specified surface type. function returns latent heat flux ; return evaporation depth paired sensible heat flux forced close \(R_n - G\). weather-station objects, method uses hum1 relative humidity present otherwise uses rh. fields interpreted relative humidity percent calculation. Available Surface Types: Temperate grassland Coniferous forest Temperate deciduous forest Tropical rain forest Cereal crops Broadleaved herbaceous crops surface_type may either Penman resistance class (Temperate grassland, Cereal crops, ...), mapped fieldClim surface class field, lawn, agriculture, coniferous forest, deciduous forest, mixed forest, shrub. example, field mapped Temperate grassland.

code: # Assuming `weather_station` is a list or data frame with a datetime field and other variables # Example weather_station object creation weather_station <- list(   datetime = as.POSIXct(c("2023-08-01 00:00", "2023-08-01 01:00", "2023-08-01 02:00")),   temp = c(15.5, 16.0, 16.5),   windspeed = c(3.2, 3.5, 3.1),   location = "Test Location"  # This will be excluded from the plot )  # Plot the temperature data plot_weather_station(weather_station, "temp")   # Plot all time series variables plot_weather_station(weather_station, NULL)

title: Minimum Distance between Climate Station and Obstacle — pos_min_dist
text: minimum distance calculated ensure climate station's measurements influenced nearby obstacles. calculation varies based whether obstacle surrounds station circularly positioned behind station. Formulas used: circularly surrounded obstacle: \(d_{min} = \pi \cdot r + 10 \cdot h\) obstacle behind station: \(h > w\): \(d_{min} = 0.5 \cdot h + 10 \cdot w\) \(h < w\): \(d_{min} = 0.5 \cdot w + 10 \cdot h\) \(h \approx w\) (within \(±5\%\)): \(d_{min} = 5 \cdot (h + w)\)

title: Incoming Diffused Radiation — rad_diffuse_in
text: diffused shortwave incoming radiation (\(D_{}\)) calculated using formula: $$D_{} = 0.5 \cdot [(1 - (1 - \text{vapor}) - (1 - \text{ozone})) \cdot SW_{toa} - SW_{}] \cdot \text{sky\_view} \cdot (1 + \cos(\theta)^2 \cdot \sin(\phi)^3)$$ : \(\text{vapor}\) vapor transmission, \(\text{ozone}\) ozone transmission, \(SW_{toa}\) shortwave radiation top atmosphere, \(SW_{}\) shortwave incoming radiation, \(\text{sky\_view}\) sky view factor, \(\theta\) terrain angle, \(\phi\) solar angle.

text: sensible heat flux (\(Q_h\)) using Bowen method calculated : $$Q_h = \frac{(R_n - G) \cdot B}{1 + B}$$ : \(R_n\) net radiation, \(G\) soil heat flux, \(B\) Bowen ratio. implemented Bowen ratio (\(B\)) calculated potential-temperature gradient absolute-humidity gradient: $$B = \gamma_{code} \cdot \frac{\Delta \theta / \Delta z}{\Delta AH / \Delta z}$$ : \(\gamma_{code} = 0.00066 \cdot (1 + 0.000946 \cdot t_1)\) empirical implementation coefficient; exact source-form equivalence remains source-open, \(\theta\) potential temperature, \(AH\) absolute humidity. inputs t1 t2 converted potential temperature temperature gradient formed. inputs hum1 hum2 relative humidity values converted internally absolute humidity humidity gradient formed. \(1 + B\) close zero, sensible heat flux can become unrealistically high. cap parameter numerical safeguard replaces near-zero denominators +/- cap. Exact closure latent_bowen() guaranteed finite uncapped denominators; capped cases guarded diagnostic outputs may close rad_bal - soil_flux exactly. Non-finite Bowen ratios denominators return NA affected elements warning.

title: Sensible heat flux by a simple bulk-transfer approach — sensible_bulk
text: implemented sensible heat flux : $$ H_{bulk} = \rho c_p \frac{T_1 - T_2}{r_a} $$ \(H_{bulk}\) sensible heat flux W m-2, \(\rho\) air density kg m-3, \(c_p\) specific heat capacity air J kg-1 K-1, \(T_1\) air temperature lower measurement height, \(T_2\) air temperature upper measurement height, \(r_a\) aerodynamic resistance s m-1. aerodynamic resistance calculated : $$ r_a = \frac{\log(z_2 / z_1)}{k \bar{u}} $$ \(z_1\) lower measurement height m, \(z_2\) upper measurement height m, \(k\) von Karman constant, \(\bar{u}\) mean wind speed m s-1. v2 supplied, \(\bar{u}\) calculated mean v1 v2. v2 supplied, v1 used \(\bar{u}\). sign convention follows package energy-balance convention: $$ H > 0 $$ means sensible heat flux away surface. Therefore, z1 < z2, positive lower-minus-upper temperature difference t1 - t2 produces positive \(H_{bulk}\). simplified neutral bulk-transfer reference. full Monin-Obukhov stability-corrected profile method. Stability corrections, roughness sublayer effects explicit surface-layer similarity functions applied . stability_method = "ri_guard", neutral estimate screened gradient Richardson number diagnostic: $$ Ri_g = \frac{g}{\bar{\theta}} \frac{\Delta \theta / \Delta z}{(\Delta u / \Delta z)^2} $$ guard rescale valid neutral fluxes. returns NA invalid stable Richardson cases attaches bulk_Ri_g bulk_stability attributes returned vector. low wind speeds make aerodynamic resistance numerically unstable. Values min_wind therefore returned NA warning. Large absolute fluxes warned using warn_threshold, capped.

text: Sensible heat flux W/m².

title: Sensible Heat using Monin-Obukhov length — sensible_monin
text: sensible heat flux (\(Q_h\)) using Monin-Obukhov method calculated : $$Q_h = - \rho \cdot c_p \cdot \frac{k \cdot u_* \cdot z_2}{\phi_h} \cdot \frac{\Delta \theta}{\Delta z}$$ : \(\rho\) air density, \(c_p\) specific heat capacity air, \(k\) von Kármán constant, \(u_*\) friction velocity, \(\phi_h\) stability correction function heat, \(\Delta \theta\) potential temperature gradient, \(\Delta z\) height difference measurements. stability correction function heat (\(\phi_h\)) calculated using gradient Richardson number (\(Ri_g\)) stability parameter (\(s_1\)). stability parameter ratio higher measurement height Monin-Obukhov length. Monin-Obukhov length values close zero, ratio can result high values, stability parameter (\(s_1\)) can capped. implemented potential-temperature gradient uses measurement-height difference \(z_2 - z_1\). Invalid heights, invalid wind speeds, invalid numerical profile states guarded elementwise return NA warning. Zero potential-temperature gradient returns zero sensible heat flux. default cap set NULL.

what: Bulk-residual turbulent heat flux workflow — turb_flux_bulk_residual
title: Bulk-residual turbulent heat flux workflow — turb_flux_bulk_residual

title: Bulk-residual turbulent heat flux workflow — turb_flux_bulk_residual
code: turb_flux_bulk_residual(weather_station, ...)

title: Bulk-residual turbulent heat flux workflow — turb_flux_bulk_residual
text: weather_station weather_station object. ... arguments passed sensible_bulk() latent_bulk_residual().

title: Bulk-residual turbulent heat flux workflow — turb_flux_bulk_residual
text: input weather_station object additional fields sensible_bulk latent_bulk_residual.

title: Bulk-residual turbulent heat flux workflow — turb_flux_bulk_residual
text: workflow combines simple bulk-transfer estimate sensible heat flux energy-balance residual latent heat flux. First, sensible heat flux estimated : $$ H_{bulk} = \rho c_p \frac{T_1 - T_2}{r_a} $$ : $$ r_a = \frac{\log(z_2 / z_1)}{k \bar{u}} $$ latent heat flux calculated : $$ LE_{res} = R_n - G - H_{bulk} $$ resulting workflow closes available turbulent energy exactly: $$ H_{bulk} + LE_{res} = R_n - G $$ package sign convention. \(R_n > 0\) net radiative input surface, \(G > 0\) heat flux soil, \(H > 0\) sensible heat flux away surface, \(LE > 0\) latent heat flux away surface. workflow intended transparent reference teaching path. full Monin-Obukhov method apply stability corrections. latent heat flux residual therefore depends directly quality rad_bal, soil_flux, bulk sensible heat estimate. Required fields weather_station : t1: lower air temperature degrees C. t2: upper air temperature degrees C. v1: lower wind speed m s-1. z1: lower measurement height m. z2: upper measurement height m. rad_bal: net radiation \(R_n\) W m-2. soil_flux: soil heat flux \(G\) W m-2. v2 present, used together v1 compute mean wind speed. v2 missing, v1 used.

title: Bulk-residual turbulent heat flux workflow — turb_flux_bulk_residual
code: ws <- build_weather_station(   datetime = as.POSIXct(     c("2023-06-01 12:00:00", "2023-06-01 12:30:00"),     tz = "UTC"   ),   t1 = c(20, 18),   t2 = c(18, 19),   v1 = c(2, 2),   v2 = c(4, 3),   z1 = 2,   z2 = 10,   rad_bal = c(400, 300),   soil_flux = c(60, 40),   elev = 100 )  out <- turb_flux_bulk_residual(ws) #> Warning: sensible_bulk: there are values above the diagnostic threshold of 600 W m-2. #> Warning: latent_bulk_residual: there are values above the diagnostic threshold of 600 W m-2.  names(out) #>  [1] "datetime"             "t1"                   "t2"                   #>  [4] "v1"                   "v2"                   "z1"                   #>  [7] "z2"                   "rad_bal"              "soil_flux"            #> [10] "elev"                 "sensible_bulk"        "latent_bulk_residual"  out_guarded <- turb_flux_bulk_residual(   ws,   stability_method = "ri_guard" ) #> Warning: sensible_bulk: there are values above the diagnostic threshold of 600 W m-2. #> Warning: sensible_bulk: very stable Richardson cases for some values; returning NA there. #> Warning: latent_bulk_residual: there are values above the diagnostic threshold of 600 W m-2.  attr(out_guarded$sensible_bulk, "bulk_stability") #> [1] "unstable"    "very_stable" attr(out_guarded$sensible_bulk, "bulk_Ri_g") #> [1] -0.1338652  0.2681879

title: Friction velocity — turb_ustar
text: function calculates friction velocity (\(u_*\)) surface, measure shear stress exerted wind surface. friction velocity important boundary layer meteorology understanding momentum transfer. friction velocity calculated using formula: $$u_* = \frac{v \cdot 0.4}{\log(z / z_0)}$$ \(v\) windspeed height anemometer, \(z\) height anemometer, \(z_0\) roughness length. roughness length (\(z_0\)) can determined based obstacle height (obs_height) type surface (surface_type).

title: fieldClim 1.2.0
text: Added Bulk-Residual workflow sensible_bulk(), latent_bulk_residual() turb_flux_bulk_residual(). sensible_bulk() estimates sensible heat flux temperature difference two heights simplified aerodynamic resistance. latent_bulk_residual() computes latent heat flux rad_bal - soil_flux - sensible. Added optional Richardson-number screening Bulk-Residual via stability_method = "ri_guard". Richardson guard attaches bulk_Ri_g bulk_stability attributes returns NA invalid, weak-shear stable profile cases. default Bulk-Residual behaviour remains neutral, unguarded estimate stability_method = "none".

title: fieldClim 1.2.0
text: Corrected Penman vapour-pressure-deficit term converting saturation actual vapour pressure hPa kPa use. fixes unit mismatch latent_penman() can substantially change Penman latent-heat estimates. Aerodynamic-resistance handling latent_penman() now elementwise; invalid log arguments, invalid wind non-finite aerodynamic states return NA warning. latent_penman.weather_station() now uses hum1 available falls back rh; missing humidity input produces explicit failure instead unclear downstream error.

title: fieldClim 1.2.0
text: Sensible latent Bowen paths now use guarded denominator logic 1 + beta. Finite, uncapped Bowen cases still close available energy rad_bal - soil_flux. Non-finite Bowen ratios non-finite denominators now return NA elementwise warnings. Near-singular capped cases treated guarded outputs interpreted exact energy-balance closure. implemented beta form remains gamma_code * dpot / dah; source-form equivalence textbook Bowen-ratio formulation remains open.

title: fieldClim 1.2.0
text: Corrected sensible_monin() use documented vertical gradient denominator z2 - z1 instead log(z2 - z1). sensible_monin() latent_monin() now return controlled values critical profile cases: zero temperature gradient gives zero sensible heat flux, zero moisture gradient gives zero latent heat flux. Invalid heights, invalid wind input, weak shear non-finite profile states return NA warnings. Monin-Obukhov/Profile outputs remain profile-based estimates. forced close rad_bal - soil_flux.

title: fieldClim 1.2.0
text: Priestley-Taylor sensible latent heat formulas remain unchanged. Warning checks made robust vectors containing NA. Documentation now clarifies sc() gam() used compatible table-scale coefficients package implementation; absolute source-unit scale remains open validation item.

title: fieldClim 1.2.0
text: sol_hour_angle() sol_azimuth() now handle POSIXct POSIXlt datetime inputs consistently. trans_air_mass_rel() now returns NA warning non-positive invalid solar elevation instead leaking NaN. Valid positive-elevation transmittance calculations keep previous formula. Radiation-balance formulas changed release.

title: fieldClim 1.2.0
text: soil_heat_flux() now returns NA warning invalid depth pairs, including non-finite, negative equal depths. Valid soil_heat_flux() calculations keep existing sign convention: positive soil_flux means heat flux soil. Corrected soil_attenuation() call soil_thermal_cond(texture, moisture) documented argument order. Soil table values source validation remain unchanged.

title: fieldClim 1.2.0
text: hum_precipitable_water() now handles POSIXct input safely converting internally POSIXlt extracting month information. fixes failures rad_sw_in() -> trans_vapor() -> hum_precipitable_water() path datetime input POSIXct. Existing POSIXlt input remains supported.
